description: This Java class `NeonDatasourceEnvironmentPostProcessor` is part of a Spring Boot application, specifically designed to configure the `spring.datasource.url` property based on environment variables that contain PostgreSQL connection URIs in Neon style. It ensures the datasource URL is set correctly before database initialization, parsing environment variables like `DATABASE_URL` or `NEON_DATABASE_URL`, and converting URI formats to JDBC URLs.

classes.name: NeonDatasourceEnvironmentPostProcessor
classes.description: A Spring Boot environment post-processor that sets the datasource URL based on Neon environment variables, converting PostgreSQL URIs to JDBC URLs.

methods.name: postProcessEnvironment
methods.description: Main method that checks existing datasource URL, retrieves environment variables, and sets the datasource property if needed.

methods.name: getOrder
methods.description: Returns the order of execution to ensure this runs early in the processing chain.

methods.name: firstNonBlank
methods.description: Utility to return the first non-blank string from variadic arguments.

methods.name: normalizeToJdbcPostgres
methods.description: Converts a PostgreSQL URI (or JDBC URL if already provided) into a normalized JDBC URL suitable for Spring Boot configuration.

methods.name: urlDecode
methods.description: Decodes URL-encoded strings using UTF-8 charset.

methods.name: urlEncode
methods.description: Encodes strings for URL query parameters using UTF-8 encoding.
